articles.title: Trump Says U.S. Military Strike Killed Leader Of Tren De Aragua Gang With Help From Venezuela
articles.content:
华盛顿（美联社讯）——特朗普总统周五表示，美国发动了一次“迅速而致命的动能打击”，击毙了赫克托·拉什福德·格雷罗·弗洛雷斯，他称之为“臭名昭著的帮派首领”。

“ Tren de Aragua已被美国列为恐怖组织。有关部门在12月宣布，格雷罗·弗洛雷斯在纽约联邦法院被起诉，罪名包括敲诈勒索阴谋罪及其他罪行，其中包括在超过十年的时间里向恐怖分子提供支持。

美国检察官杰伊·克莱顿当时表示，该团伙对北美、南美和欧洲无数的暴力事件、敲诈勒索和毒品走私活动负有责任。特朗普周四提名克莱顿出任国家情报总监。

美国国务院曾悬赏最高500万美元征集有助于逮捕格雷罗·弗洛雷斯的情报。

特朗普在他的社交媒体网站上发帖称：“Tren de Aragua恐怖分子在委内瑞拉或其他任何地方都再无安全庇护所，在我的领导下，我们将随时随地在任何地方找到这些凶残的杀手和毒枭，并将他们送入他们应去的地狱深处。”特朗普的帖子用别名“尼诺·格雷罗”来称呼格雷罗·弗洛雷斯。

五角大楼除了特朗普在Truth Social上的帖子外没有补充任何内容。委内瑞拉通讯部没有立即回应就此次行动置评的请求。

特朗普对该帮派采取了一系列极端行动，包括对其政府指控向美国走私毒品的船只进行系列打击。自9月初开始打击所谓的“毒品恐怖分子”以来，美国军方已在东太平洋和加勒比海地区的船只行动中击毙至少207人。

特朗普和政府官员一直将Tren de Aragua视为困扰美国部分城市的暴力和非法毒品交易的根源。总统数月来反复声称——尽管一份解密的美国情报评估对此提出反驳——Tren de Aragua是在委内瑞拉总统尼古拉斯·马杜罗的控制下运作的。今年1月，美国将马杜罗带离委内瑞拉，面临美国毒品指控。

Tren de Aragua十多年前起源于委内瑞拉中部阿拉瓜州一个臭名昭著的无法无天的监狱。随着数百万委内瑞拉人迁移到其他拉丁美洲国家或美国寻找更好的生活条件，该帮派近年来不断扩大。

该帮派的规模尚不清楚。拥有大量委内瑞拉移民的国家（包括秘鲁和哥伦比亚）指控该组织在本地区制造了一系列暴力事件。然而，据追踪拉丁美洲犯罪的智库InSight Crime称，与哥伦比亚、中美洲和巴西的其他犯罪组织不同，Tren de Aragua并未大规模参与国际可卡因走私。

在委内瑞拉，帮派头目长期以来一直参与各种非法活动，包括金矿开采。

特朗普在竞选连任时承诺打击移民和犯罪。根据美联社-诺克罗公共事务研究中心的调查，尽管民调显示他在处理经济问题上的支持率有所下降，但移民问题仍是特朗普最强的议题。

美联社驻墨西哥城记者雷吉娜·加西亚·卡诺对此文有贡献。
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Friday that a “swift and lethal kinetic” U.S. strike has killed Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, whom he called “the infamous leader” of the Tren de Aragua gang.

Tren de Aragua has been labeled by the United States as a terrorist organization. Guerrero Flores was charged in a New York federal court with racketeering conspiracy and other crimes, including lending support to terrorists in crimes that stretched more than a decade, authorities announced in December.

Advertisement

U.S. Attorney Jay Clayton said at the time that the gang is responsible for countless acts of violence, extortion and drug trafficking in North America, South America and Europe. Trump nominated Clayton on Thursday to be director of national intelligence.

The U.S. State Department had offered rewards of up to $5 million for information leading to Guerrero Flores’ arrest

In a post on his social media site, Trump wrote, “Tren de Aragua terrorists no longer have safe haven in Venezuela or anywhere else and, under my leadership, we will find these vicious murderers and drug lords anytime, anyplace, and send them to the depths of hell where they belong.” Trump’s post referred to Guerrero Flores by his alias, “Niño Guerrero.”

Advertisement

The Pentagon had nothing to add beyond the Truth Social post from Trump. Venezuela’s ministry of communications did not immediately respond to a request for comment on the operation.

Trump has taken a series of extraordinary actions against the gang, including a series of strikes on small boats his administration has accused of smuggling drugs to America. At least 207 people have been killed in boat strikes by the U.S. military in the eastern Pacific Ocean and Caribbean Sea since the Trump administration began targeting those it calls “narcoterrorists” in early September.

Trump and administration officials have consistently blamed Tren de Aragua for being at the root of the violence and illicit drug dealing that plague some U.S. cities. The president spent months repeating the claim — contradicted by a declassified U.S. intelligence assessment — that Tren de Aragua had operated under Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro’s control. The U.S. whisked Maduro out of Venezuela to face U.S. drug charges in January.

Advertisement

Tren de Aragua originated more than a decade ago at an infamously lawless prison with hardened criminals in Venezuela’s central state of Aragua. The gang has expanded in recent years as millions of Venezuelans migrated to other Latin American countries or the U.S. in search of better living conditions.

The size of the gang is unclear. Countries with large populations of Venezuelan migrants, including Peru and Colombia, have accused the group of being behind a spree of violence in the region. Still, unlike other criminal organizations from Colombia, Central America and Brazil, Tren de Aragua has no large-scale involvement in smuggling cocaine across international borders, according to InSight Crime, a think tank that tracks crime across Latin America.

In Venezuela, gang leaders have long been known to participate in various illegal activities, including gold mining.

Advertisement

Trump campaigned for a second term promising to crack down on immigration and crime. While polls show his favorability ratings have sagged on his handling of the economy, immigration remains Trump’s strongest issue, according to the AP-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research.

__

Associated Press writer Regina Garcia Cano in Mexico City contributed.

Related
Donald TrumpMilitaryVenezuelasouth americaTren De Aragua

articles.title: U.S. And Iran Have Agreed To Wording Of A Deal To End Their War, Pakistan's Prime Minister Says
articles.content:
伊斯兰堡（美联社）——巴基斯坦总理周五表示，美国和伊朗已就结束中东战争的协议措辞达成一致，调解人员正在与双方合作敲定最终协议。

总理夏巴兹·谢里夫表示，美国和伊朗已达成“最终商定的文本”。他说，率先进行调解努力的巴基斯坦正在与交战国就下一步行动进行合作。

谢里夫在X平台上发帖称：“和平从未像现在这样接近。”

本周伊朗与美国和以色列交换火力三天后，谈判出现明显突破，中东面临全面战争的威胁。

伊朗外交部长阿巴斯·阿拉格希周五在X平台上发帖称，协议“从未如此接近”。美国总统特朗普近几周多次表示两国即将达成协议，他在自己的社交媒体上转发了阿拉格希的帖子。

美国和以色列于2月28日发动的战争震动中东，几乎关闭了波斯湾的石油和天然气运输。自4月7日以来，停火协议一直脆弱维持。

**伊朗官员称核细节将在结束战争协议后跟进**

阿拉格希告诉伊朗国家电视台周五，双方正在努力签署一项初步协议，宣布在“所有战线，包括黎巴嫩”结束战争。

以色列自3月初以来一直在与伊朗盟友真主党在黎巴嫩作战。以色列不是美国和伊朗之间谈判的一方，其领导人表示他们不打算从黎巴嫩撤军。

阿拉格希表示，处理伊朗核计划的条款将在初步协议签署后的60天内敲定。他说，各方可以同意延长这一期限。

伊朗的核计划一直是关键分歧点。美国和以色列担心这可能导致核武器——这是其领导人发动战争的主要原因。伊朗坚称其核活动用于和平目的。

一位不愿透露姓名的美国政府高级官员在白宫设定的条件下向记者通报情况时表示，即将达成的协议将开始销毁或移除伊朗高浓缩铀的进程。

这位官员表示，双方签署协议后的60天期限将用于制定移除伊朗浓缩铀的技术细节。这位官员没有详细说明美国设想由谁来负责移除这些铀，这些铀据信被封存在去年遭美国轰炸的三个核设施地下。

同样关键的是伊朗实际封锁霍尔木兹海峡——这条石油和天然气的重要航运通道。海峡运输中断影响了全球能源供应，推高了燃油价格，并使该地区以外的食品和其他必需品价格上涨。

这位美国官员表示，即将达成的协议包括重新开放海峡的条款。

阿拉格希表示，伊朗希望达成一项允许德黑兰对通过霍尔木兹海峡的船只“收取服务费”的协议。伊朗在战争期间实施了收费制度，美国和其他国家称这违反了国际法。

阿拉格希说：“将涉及费用，这些费用必须支付。”

**官员称协议可能在未来几天签署**

三位地区官员表示，即将达成的协议预计还包括逐步解除对伊朗制裁和解冻伊朗资产。这些官员因谈判的敏感性而要求匿名。

他们表示，预计在华盛顿和德黑兰的官员批准后，将在未来几天举行协议签署仪式。

特朗普周四声称谈判取得重大进展，就在几个小时前他还威胁要升级攻击并夺取伊朗石油产业。

以色列总理本雅明·内塔尼亚胡表示，以色列不是正在谈判的协议的一方。他周五在一份声明中表示，他与特朗普“完全同意”伊朗不能拥有核武器。

以色列国防部长以色列·卡茨在另一份声明中表示，以色列也期望特朗普维护以色列的关键利益，包括削弱伊朗的导弹计划和代理人网络。

卡茨警告称，以色列仍可能单独对伊朗采取行动，该国不会从黎巴嫩、叙利亚和加沙占领区撤出，也不会从以色列占领的约旦河西岸北部难民营撤出。

这些地区官员表示，这项协议主要由巴基斯坦斡旋，由陆军参谋长阿西姆·穆尼尔元帅领导，并得到沙特阿拉伯、土耳其、埃及和卡塔尔的支持。

——

普赖斯来自华盛顿，比纳姆来自佐治亚州萨凡纳。美联社记者萨哈尔·阿梅里（柏林）、朱莉娅·弗兰克尔（耶路撒冷）以及科林·宾克利和阿梅尔·马哈尼（华盛顿）也作出了贡献。
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

ISLAMABAD (AP) — Pakistan’s prime minister said Friday the United States and Iran have agreed to wording of an agreement aimed at ending their war in the Middle East and that mediators were working with both sides to finalize a deal.

Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif said the U.S. and Iran have reached a “final, agreed upon text.” He said Pakistan, which has taken the lead in mediation efforts, was working with the warring countries on next steps.

Advertisement

“Peace has never been this close as it is now,” Sharif said in a post on X.

The apparent breakthrough in negotiations comes after Iran exchanged fire with the U.S. and Israel over three days this week, threatening to return the Middle East to full-scale war.

Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said Friday an agreement “has never been closer” in a post on X. U.S. President Donald Trump, who has said multiple times in recent weeks the countries are on the cusp of a deal, shared Araghchi’s post on his own social media.

The war launched by the U.S. and Israel on Feb. 28 has rattled the Middle East and virtually shut down oil and natural gas shipments from the Persian Gulf. A fragile ceasefire has been in place since April 7.

Advertisement

Iranian Official Says Nuclear Details Will Follow An Agreement To End The War

Araghchi told Iranian state TV on Friday that both sides were working toward signing an initial agreement declaring an end to the war “on all fronts, including Lebanon.”

Israel has been fighting the Iranian-allied militia Hezbollah in Lebanon since early March. Israel is not a party to the negotiations between the U.S. and Iran, and its leaders have said they don’t plan to withdraw from Lebanon.

Advertisement

Araghchi said terms dealing with Iran’s nuclear program would be finalized in the 60 days after the initial agreement is signed. He said the parties could agree to extend that period.

Iran’s nuclear program has been a key point of division. The U.S. and Israel fear it could lead to an atomic weapon — a main reason their leaders cited for going to war. Tehran has insisted its nuclear efforts are for peaceful purposes.

A senior U.S. administration official, who briefed reporters on condition of anonymity under ground rules set by the White House, said Friday that the emerging agreement would begin the process of destroying or removing Tehran’s highly enriched uranium.

Advertisement

The official said the 60-day period after both sides sign the deal would be used to work out technical details for removing Iran’s enriched uranium. The official did not detail who the U.S. envisions taking charge of removing the uranium, which is believed to entombed under three nuclear sites that were battered by U.S. strikes last year.

Also critical is Iran’s effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a vital shipping lane for oil and natural gas. Disruption of transit through the strait has crimped global energy supplies, driven up fuel prices and made food and other basics more expensive well beyond the region.

The U.S. official said the emerging agreement includes provisions for reopening the strait.

Araghchi said Iran wants a deal that allows Tehran to charge ships “for services rendered” when they transit the Strait of Hormuz. Iran has imposed a toll system during the war, which the U.S. and other nations say violates international law.

Advertisement

“There will be costs involved,” Araghchi said, “and those costs must be paid.”

Officials Say A Deal Could Be Signed In The Coming Days

Three regional officials said the emerging deal is also expected to include the phased lifting of sanctions on Iran and the release of frozen Iranian assets. The officials spoke on condition of anonymity because of the sensitivity of the negotiations.

They said they expect a signing ceremony for the agreement in the coming days after officials in Washington and Tehran approve it.

Advertisement

Trump on Thursday claimed significant progress in the negotiations, just hours after he threatened to escalate attacks and seize Iran’s oil industry.

Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has said Israel is not a party to the deal being negotiated. He said in a statement Friday that he and Trump were in “full agreement” that Iran must not have nuclear weapons.

Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz said in a separate statement that Israel also expects Trump to uphold key Israeli interests, including weakening Iran’s missile program and proxy network.

Advertisement

Katz warned that Israel could still act independently toward Iran and that the country would not pull out of the zones it is occupying in Lebanon, Syria and Gaza, nor would it withdraw from the northern refugee camps of the Israeli-occupied West Bank.

The deal was largely being brokered by Pakistan, led by its army chief Field Marshal Asim Munir, the regional officials said, with backing from Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Egypt, and Qatar.

___

Price reported from Washington and Bynum reported from Savannah, Georgia. Associated Press journalists Sahar Ameri in Berlin, Julia Frankel in Jerusalem, and Collin Binkley and Aamer Madhani in Washington contributed.

Advertisement

Related

Iran war

Pakistan

Strait Hormuz

Trump Pulls Back, U.S. And Iran Agree To 2-Week Ceasefire
Israel’s Dangerous War Against Iran Is Escalating — With Donald Trump’s Tacit Blessing
Trump Claims 'Peace' With Iran Will Come While Threatening Further War

articles.title: U.S.-Iran Peace Memorandum Could Be Signed On Sunday In Geneva: Reuters
articles.content:
迪拜/华盛顿/巴黎，6月12日（路透社）——一位西方消息人士周五对路透社表示，美国与伊朗之间的海湾战争停战备忘录可能最快于周日签署，日内瓦正成为最可能的签署地点。

该消息人士称，备忘录的措辞仍在最终敲定中，伊朗坚持其立场，即协议必须同时结束黎巴嫩的战斗——以色列一直在那里与伊朗支持的黎巴嫩真主党民兵作战。

目标是周六前敲定措辞，以便协议能由美国副总统万斯和伊朗议会议长穆罕默德·巴格尔·卡利巴夫签署。尚未确定地点，但日内瓦正成为最可能的选择。

特朗普周四表示，由于协议已经就绪，他将取消对伊朗的新一轮打击。

“我们刚刚与伊朗达成了一项重大停战协议，”特朗普周四在白宫对记者表示。

但伊朗官员周五描述的协议条款似乎满足了伊朗迄今为止的大部分要求，而特朗普除了重新开放霍尔木兹海峡外，似乎几乎没有获得他寻求的任何东西。伊朗在特朗普2月下令发动袭击后封锁了霍尔木兹海峡。

一位伊朗高级消息人士周五对路透社表示，草案将免除对伊朗石油的制裁，解冻数十亿美元资金，并要求所有战线停火，包括黎巴嫩。

核问题将被搁置，留待后续谈判。华盛顿希望达成一项确保伊朗永不发展核武器的协议；伊朗表示其并非在寻求核武器。

解除制裁、解冻伊朗资产和停止以色列对黎巴嫩的攻击是伊朗的核心要求。该消息人士未提及伊朗可能提供的回报。美国方面没有立即回应。

伊朗梅赫尔通讯社称，条款还包括其他关键的美方让步，包括承诺从伊朗周边撤军，并提出重建被摧毁的伊朗经济的计划。

梅赫尔通讯社的报道称：“美国及其盟友必须提交价值至少3000亿美元的伊朗重建计划。”

**“重大协议”**

特朗普在周四晚间再次威胁要“狠狠打击”伊朗数小时后宣布达成协议，此举推动周五全球股市上涨，油价下跌。布伦特原油价格在欧洲早盘交易中下跌超过2%。

这场战争始于2月28日美国和以色列对伊朗的打击。在整个战争期间，特朗普曾多次发表类似声明称协议即将达成，但最终都没有实现。

但市场从他的最新言论中得到安慰，认为这标志着特别紧张的几天升级局势的结束。这轮升级始于伊朗和以色列自4月停火以来首次交火，并持续了美国对伊朗进行两天打击和伊朗回击美国地区基地。

“海峡将在我们签署后立即正式开放，可能很快，非常快，也许在欧洲过周末，”特朗普说，并补充说万斯将出席协议签署。他没有详细说明。

当被问及伊朗最高领袖阿亚图拉·穆吉塔巴·哈梅内伊是否批准了该协议时，特朗普说：“我理解答案是肯定的。”

**美军击落2架伊朗无人机**

伊朗媒体报道称，外交部发言人埃斯梅伊尔·巴加伊周四表示，协议的大部分内容已经敲定，但伊朗不会在其红线问题上妥协。

一位美国官员周四表示，霍尔木兹海峡周边紧张局势仍然高企，在德黑兰试图打击过境这一重要水道的商船后，美军击落了两架伊朗单向攻击无人机。

伊朗国家媒体报道称，伊朗军方阻止了一艘油轮通过海峡，并报道了周五早些时候的爆炸声。

这场冲突已成为白宫的政治难题。民调显示，在选民对高油价愤怒之际，特朗普的支持率正在下滑。

一些共和党人公开担心，这场战争的不受欢迎可能导致他们在11月的中期选举中失去对国会的控制。

对以色列来说，限制黎巴嫩的战斗可能难以接受。以色列2月与美国一起发动了这场战争，但没有被纳入和平谈判。

以色列总理本雅明·内塔尼亚胡的办公室在一份声明中表示，以色列不是与伊朗的任何谅解备忘录的当事方。

（路透社分社报道；林肯·费斯特和加雷斯·琼斯撰稿；克拉伦斯·费尔南德斯和彼得·格拉夫编辑）
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

DUBAI/WASHINGTON/PARIS, June 12 (Reuters) - A memorandum between the United States and Iran to halt the war in the Gulf could be signed as soon as Sunday, a Western source told Reuters on Friday, with Geneva emerging as the likeliest venue.

The source said language in the memorandum was still being finalized and Iran was sticking to its position that the deal must also end fighting in Lebanon, where Israel has been battling against the Iran-backed Hezbollah militia.

Advertisement

The aim was to finalize the wording by Saturday so the agreement could be signed by U.S. Vice President JD Vance and Iran’s Parliament Speaker Mohammed Baqer Qalibaf. No venue had been established but Geneva was emerging as the likeliest.

Trump said on Thursday he was calling off new strikes on Iran because the deal was now ready.

“We just made a great settlement of the war with Iran,” Trump told reporters in the White House on Thursday.

Advertisement

But the terms of the deal as described on Friday by Iranian officials appear to offer Tehran much of what it has demanded so far, with Trump appearing to win little of what he has sought, beyond the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, which Iran shut after he ordered attacks in February.

A senior Iranian source told Reuters on Friday that the draft would waive sanctions on Iran’s oil, unfreeze billions of dollars of its funds, and require a cessation of hostilities on all fronts, including in Lebanon.

Advertisement

Nuclear issues would be set aside for later talks. Washington wants a deal to ensure that Iran never develops a nuclear weapon; Iran says it is not seeking one.

The waiving of sanctions, unfreezing of Iranian assets and halt to Israeli attacks on Lebanon are essential Iranian demands. The source made no mention of what Iran might offer in return. There was no immediate response from the United States.

Iran’s Mehr news agency said the terms also included other key U.S. concessions, including a commitment to withdraw its forces from around Iran and present a plan for rebuilding the shattered Iranian economy.

Advertisement

“The United States and its allies must submit plans for Iran’s reconstruction worth at least $300 billion,” the Mehr report said.

‘Great Settlement’

Trump’s announcement of a deal — hours after he threatened again to hit Iran “very hard” on Thursday night — prompted global shares to rally and oil prices to slip on Friday. Brent crude prices were down more than 2% in European morning trade.

Throughout the war, which began on February 28 with U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran, Trump has made similar declarations that a deal was at hand, only for no deal to emerge.

Advertisement

But markets took comfort that his latest words signaled the end of a particularly tense few days of escalation, which began with Iran and Israel trading fire for the first time since an April ceasefire, and continued through two days of U.S. strikes on Iran and Iranian return fire at U.S. regional bases.

“The strait will officially open as soon as we sign, which could be soon, very soon, maybe over the weekend in Europe,” Trump said, adding that Vance would attend the deal signing. He did not elaborate.

Advertisement

Asked if Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei had approved the deal, Trump said, “I understand the answer is yes.”

U.S. Forces Down 2 Iranian Drones

Iranian media reported Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei as saying on Thursday large parts of the agreement had been finalized, but Iran would not compromise on its red lines.

Tension remained high around the Strait of Hormuz, with U.S. forces shooting down two Iranian one-way attack drones after Tehran attempted to strike commercial ships transiting the vital waterway, a U.S. official said on Thursday.

Advertisement

Iran’s military stopped a tanker from transiting the strait, state media said, reporting the sound of explosions early on Friday.

The conflict has become a political headache for the White House, with polls showing Trump’s approval ratings sinking amid voter anger over high gasoline prices.

Some Republicans have openly worried that the war’s unpopularity could cost them control of Congress in November’s midterm elections.

Advertisement

Curbs on fighting in Lebanon could be difficult to accept for Israel, which started the war alongside the United States in February but has not been included in peace negotiations.

Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s office said in a statement that Israel was not a party to any memorandum of understanding with Iran.

(Reporting by Reuters bureaus; Writing by Lincoln Feast and Gareth Jones; Editing by Clarence Fernandez and Peter Graff)
